consumer_contexts: grid, list, weight, and capacity inventories across different game genres
player backpacks, shared storage, equipment sources, and service-owned item containers
common_kernel: stable item definitions and instance identity
container capacity, stacking, and atomic mutations
snapshots, persistence contracts, and change observations
renderer-neutral presentation and optional engine and XR adapters
configurable_variation: slot, weight, capacity, uniqueness, sorting, filtering, and presentation policies
consumer-owned item effects, equipment, crafting, economy, networking, and art
excluded_project_specific: one game's item names, lore, economy values, drop tables, recipes, and art
one project's scenes, commands, services, namespaces, folder layout, and private assemblies
rationale: Inventory is a widely recurring game-system family with a stable container and mutation kernel; genre-specific economy, content, UI, networking, and item behavior remain configurable or consumer-owned.
non_claims: Admission does not claim that every inventory genre, renderer, network model, economy, or device is implemented or verified.
